fix: robust unique-art deduplication using stored tcgId (#50)

* Initial plan

it only handled 6 known TCGPlayer price types and would silently fail for
others (e.g. shadowlessNormal, unlimitedNormal). Add a `tcgId` field to

bleed between All Variants and Unique Art Only modes.

Bump CARD_CACHE_VERSION to 5 to invalidate stale cached card data.

Fix notes text not wrapping before button area stretches (#30)

Long notes text was pushing the right-side container wider than the
rating buttons row, instead of word-wrapping within the buttons' width.

The right-side container was a plain `<div>` whose width was determined
by its widest child. When notes exceeded the buttons row width, the
entire container grew.

- **`vote-song-list.tsx`**: Make the right-side container `flex w-min
flex-col items-end` — `w-min` (`width: min-content`) constrains the
column width to the `shrink-0` rating buttons' intrinsic width, forcing
note text to word-wrap within that width
- **`note-editor.tsx`**: Add `self-stretch` to both display and editing
containers so they fill the column width, and add `break-words` to the
note paragraph for safety with very long words

commit.message:
refactor(jetson): migrate from Docker to host-native deployment

NVIDIA does not provide an official JetPack 6 Docker base image with
TRT 10.x + Python 3.10. The existing Dockerfile targets JetPack 5
(TRT 8.5, Python 3.8, CUDA 11.4) which is incompatible with JetPack 6
hardware. TensorRT engines are not portable across TRT versions.

Changes:
- setup_jetson.sh: Remove Docker build/run, add Python/TRT/PyCUDA
  verification, generate host-native systemd service with sandbox
  hardening (NoNewPrivileges, ProtectSystem=strict, PrivateTmp)
- setup_jetson.sh: Replace world-writable Docker perms (777/666)
  with standard user perms (755/644), add final chown after model
  conversion to fix root-owned files
- Dockerfile: Mark as DEPRECATED with explanation
- requirements.txt: Bump flask 3.1.3, waitress 3.0.2 (host Python 3.10)
- agrikd.service: Update template comments
- app/main.py, app/gui_app.py: Update path resolution comments
- All docs updated: jetson_deployment_guide.md, project_instruction.md,
  build_plan.md, project_context.md, docs/README.md

commit.message:
fix: remove erroneous PR subtraction from issue counts; clamp negatives in display

The issues.listForRepo API returns only issues (not PRs) in its
response, so subtracting PR counts produced large negative issue values
(e.g. -468, -619) which Chart.js rendered as bars extending to the
left, creating an unintended butterfly/diverging chart appearance.

Changes:
- collectIssueCounts: drop the two pulls.list calls and remove the
  subtraction; use the raw issues API counts directly (+ Math.max(0)
  guard for safety)
- build-pages.ts: clamp r.issues.open / r.issues.closed to >= 0 when
  aggregating totals and building topRepos, so any existing cached or
  fixture data with negative values is rendered correctly
- issues.test.ts: update tests to reflect the new single-API approach

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix OpenAPI publish job being skipped on manual workflow dispatch (#45)

https://github.com/SubDynamics/event-hub-api/pull/45

The OpenAPI workflow’s `publish-types-package` job could be skipped on
manual runs due to an overly strict branch gate and string-based input
check. This updates job gating so manual dispatch behaves independently
from push-to-main change detection.

- **Condition split by trigger type**
- Kept push behavior scoped to `main` + detected `packages/types/**`
changes.
- Separated manual dispatch logic so it no longer depends on `github.ref
== refs/heads/main`.

- **Typed manual input evaluation**
- Switched from `github.event.inputs.publish == 'true'` to
`inputs.publish` to align with boolean `workflow_dispatch` inputs.

- **Resulting job predicate**
  ```yaml
  if: >
    (github.event_name == 'push' &&
    github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' &&
    needs.generate-and-validate.outputs.types_changed == 'true') ||
    (github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' && inputs.publish)
  ```

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use Style::default()+DIM for overlay text to avoid ANSI color remapping

Replace Color::White and MUTED in overlays with terminal-adaptive styles:
- Primary text: Style::default() (inherits terminal's configured fg color)
- Secondary text: Modifier::DIM (dims relative to terminal's native color)

Affected: SelectionList labels/descs/markers, InfoPanel help lines (commands,
tips), status overlay values, and all overlay border hint text.

than true-color Rgb(140,140,140), producing inverted visual hierarchy.
